title: Ectasis turgida
intro: Ectasis turgida, also known as Eclipta, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Americas. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting liver function due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Eclipta include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Eclipta is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical applications.
taxonomy: Ectasis turgida is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. The Acanthaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the acanthus family or the acanthoid family. This family is native to tropical, subtropical, and warm temperate regions around the world. The Acanthaceae family consists of over 2,000 species, including herbs, shrubs, and small trees. These plants are known for their often fragrant flowers and showy inflorescences.
